name: Persistence Test abc123
description: A test protocol for verifying persistence

name: Test
description: This protocol guides the risk stratification and management of fever without source (FWS) in children aged 3-36 months, based on current AAP guidelines.

questions.questionText: Does the child exhibit lethargy, poor perfusion, cyanosis, or respiratory distress (toxic appearance)?
Has the child received at least 2 doses of Hib and PCV13/15 vaccines?
Does the patient have Sickle Cell Disease, a central line, or is immunocompromised?
Is Urine Analysis (UA) positive for infection?
Is Gram stain positive for bacteria from CSF or Blood (if performed)?
Is the patient unable to maintain adequate hydration orally?

severityRules.detail: Child exhibits signs of toxicity (lethargy, poor perfusion, cyanosis, or respiratory distress).
Patient has Sickle Cell Disease, a central line, or is immunocompromised.
Significant lab abnormalities (Procalcitonin >0.5ng/mL, positive UA, or positive Gram stain) indicate high risk.

severityRules.condition: !toxicAppearance && !comorbidities && !(procalcitoninResult > 0.5 || uaPositive === true || gramStainPositive === true) && (temperature >= 39.0 || immunizedHibPcv === false)
severityRules.detail: Nontoxic, no comorbidities or significant lab abnormalities, but temperature is ≥39.0°C OR the child is under-immunized.

severityRules.condition: !toxicAppearance && !comorbidities && !(procalcitoninResult > 0.5 || uaPositive === true || gramStainPositive === true) && temperature < 39.0 && immunizedHibPcv === true
severityRules.detail: Nontoxic, immunized, temperature <39.0°C, and no comorbidities or significant lab abnormalities.

management.title: Required Lab Orders (Moderate Risk)
recommendations: Urine Analysis (UA) and Urine Culture (UCult) - Priority 1
Consider Complete Blood Count (CBC) with differential

management.title: Required Lab Orders (Low Risk)
recommendations: Consider Urine Analysis (UA) and Urine Culture (UCult) for females or uncircumcised males.

disposition.text: Admission for all High Risk patients for further evaluation and management, including those with Procalcitonin >0.5ng/mL.
Consider admission if patient has positive UA/Gram stain and is unable to maintain adequate hydration orally, even if not classified as severe risk.
Discharge home with reliable 24-hour follow-up instructions.
Discharge home for Moderate Risk patients with negative UA and reliable 24-hour follow-up instructions.

redFlags: Unable to wake up or severe lethargy

drugDoses.notes: For empiric treatment of suspected serious bacterial infection.
Administer 50-100 mg/kg/day divided into three daily doses (TID) for confirmed UTI.

references.title: Evaluation and Management of Febrile Infants and Toddlers (AAP Clinical Practice Guideline)
